summary:
**Claim**: There is no detectable acid-base disturbance in this patient.  
**Evidence**: pH (7.40) and PaCO₂ (40 mmHg) are within normal ranges; serum bicarbonate (25 mEq/L) falls within physiological limits (22–26). Vomiting could theoretically induce metabolic alkalosis via gastric acid loss, but no significant deviation in pH or HCO₃ occurs, indicating effective homeostatic compensation.  
**Answer**: C  

(498 tokens)
